verses.1: God, give the king your justice;
verses.2: He will judge your people with righteousness,
verses.3: The mountains shall bring prosperity to the people.
verses.4: He will judge the poor of the people.
verses.5: They shall fear you while the sun endures;
verses.6: He will come down like rain on the mown grass,
verses.7: In his days, the righteous shall flourish,
verses.8: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea,
verses.9: Those who dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him.
verses.10: The kings of Tarshish and of the islands will bring tribute.
verses.11: Yes, all kings shall fall down before him.
verses.12: For he will deliver the needy when he cries;
verses.13: He will have pity on the poor and needy.
verses.14: He will redeem their soul from oppression and violence.
verses.15: He will live; and Sheba’s gold will be given to him.
verses.16: Abundance of grain shall be throughout the land.
verses.18: Praise be to the LORD God, the God of Israel,
verses.19: Blessed be his glorious name forever!
verses.20: This ends the prayers by David, the son of Jesse.
